subject: [PATCH v5 3/4] cleanup: Annotate guard constructors with nonnull

reviews.author: Miguel Ojeda
reviews.summary: Confirmed that __nonnull_args() is intended to annotate non-null arguments, not the return value. No objections or concerns raised.
reviews.raw_body:
On Tue, Jun 2, 2026 at 9:13 AM Dmitry Ilvokhin <d@ilvokhin.com> wrote:
>
> Miguel, I dropped your Acked-by due to the rename. Went with
> __nonnull_args() (over __knonnull()). Happy to restore your tag if that
> spelling works for you.

I am fine with either, but thanks for the caution! :)

I assume `_args()` is meant as "the `nonnull` for the arguments, not
the return"?

Cheers,
Miguel

2026-06-02.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new macro, __nonnull_args(), to annotate guard constructors in the Linux kernel. The goal is to warn the compiler when NULL is statically known to be passed to these constructors. This is achieved by re-declaring the constructor with __nonnull_args() or annotating it directly.

2026-06-03.patch_summary: This patch series introduces a new macro, __nonnull_args(), to annotate guard constructors in the Linux kernel. The goal is to warn the compiler when NULL is statically known to be passed to these constructors. This is achieved by re-declaring the constructor with __nonnull_args() or annotating it directly.
